label: PLUMBING-06 Roto-Rooter invoice (phone photo of paper)

lineItems.description: Line item 1 - Grease and obstruction removal/testing

redFlags: Service date shown as 2/2/2/26 — illegible or incorrectly formatted date
Much of the invoice is redacted or obscured by purple highlighting, preventing full verification of scope and details
No hourly labor rate disclosed — only flat labor total visible
Line item descriptions partially illegible due to redaction

No state plumbing license number on the quote. Plumbing is licensed statewide in 45+ states. Unlicensed plumbing work voids insurance claims and can't be permitted or inspected. AARP lists unlicensed home services as a top-5 elder fraud category.
data.summary: Roto-Rooter drain cleaning service in Indianapolis with a total invoice of $982.80 (labor $560.00). Significant portions of the invoice are redacted or obscured, limiting ability to verify the full scope of work, parts costs, and warranty details. The labor cost appears high for the described work (grease and obstruction removal), but without clear hourly rates or itemized parts, fairness cannot be assessed. Recommend requesting a clear, unredacted copy of the invoice to verify all charges.

data.fixture: 60 Gallon Bradford white indirect water heater

lineItems.description: Drain and disconnect supply and return loop going from boiler to existing water heater
Disconnect domestic cold water and hot water connections from existing water heater
Clean and disposal of old water heater
Install 60 Gallon Bradford white indirect water heater
Repipe supply and return from boiler and also hot and cold domestic water connections
Flush and purge supply and return coil on new water heater to get the air out for proper heating
Wire aqua-stat for proper communication between tank and boiler

redFlags: No pipe material type specified for repipe work (supply/return and domestic connections)
No permit information mentioned
No warranty details provided
Labor and parts costs not separated or itemized
Page 1 of 3 — full quote details may be on subsequent pages and are not visible

Repipe quote without specifying PEX-A vs PEX-B vs copper Type L/M is unenforceable. PEX-A (expansion) has a 25-year warranty; PEX-B crimp can dezincify in aggressive water. Type M copper is the thinnest allowed and not ideal. Price difference is 2-3x between bottom and top spec.

No state plumbing license number on the quote. Plumbing is licensed statewide in 45+ states. Unlicensed plumbing work voids insurance claims and can't be permitted or inspected. AARP lists unlicensed home services as a top-5 elder fraud category.

Whole-house repipe requires opening 20-80 access holes in drywall. If drywall patch/texture/paint is not in the scope, the homeowner is stuck finding a separate drywall contractor for $1,500-$5,000 of additional work.
data.summary: This is a water heater replacement quote for a 60-gallon Bradford indirect water heater at $6,950.00. The scope includes removal of old heater, installation of new unit, and complete repipe of supply/return and domestic hot/cold connections. However, the quote lacks critical details: no hourly labor rate, no itemized parts costs, no warranty information, no pipe material specification, and no permit mention. The all-inclusive subtotal obscures whether pricing is competitive. This appears to be page 1 of a 3-page document, so additional details may exist. Recommend requesting a detailed breakdown of labor hours, parts costs, material specifications, and warranty terms before accepting.

detectedUpsells.notes: Only if water quality test shows issues.
Required by code in some areas with closed water systems.

structuredRedFlags.name: no_material_spec_repipe
structuredRedFlags.explanation: Repipe quote without specifying PEX-A vs PEX-B vs copper Type L/M is unenforceable. PEX-A (expansion) has a 25-year warranty; PEX-B crimp can dezincify in aggressive water. Type M copper is the thinnest allowed and not ideal. Price difference is 2-3x between bottom and top spec.
structuredRedFlags.action: Require: manufacturer + type (e.g., 'Uponor AquaPEX PEX-A, 1/2" and 3/4"') and total linear feet.

structuredRedFlags.name: no_license_number
structuredRedFlags.explanation: No state plumbing license number on the quote. Plumbing is licensed statewide in 45+ states. Unlicensed plumbing work voids insurance claims and can't be permitted or inspected. AARP lists unlicensed home services as a top-5 elder fraud category.
structuredRedFlags.action: Verify the state plumbing license number before any deposit. Most state boards have free online lookup.

structuredRedFlags.name: repipe_no_drywall_repair
structuredRedFlags.explanation: Whole-house repipe requires opening 20-80 access holes in drywall. If drywall patch/texture/paint is not in the scope, the homeowner is stuck finding a separate drywall contractor for $1,500-$5,000 of additional work.
structuredRedFlags.action: Scope must specify whether access-hole drywall repair is included, and to what finish level (patched only, textured, textured + painted).

tierData.notes: Contractor-exclusive (Bradford White is not sold to DIY). 10-year warranty, heavier steel, better anodes.

No state plumbing license number on the quote. Plumbing is licensed statewide in 45+ states. Unlicensed plumbing work voids insurance claims and can't be permitted or inspected. AARP lists unlicensed home services as a top-5 elder fraud category.
data.summary: This is a straightforward 50-gallon gas water heater replacement quote totaling $1,380. The Rheem Performance model is a solid mid-range choice with a 6-year tank warranty. Labor is reasonably priced at $130/hour for 2.5 hours of work. Permit and disposal are included, which is good. However, the quote lacks specifics on gas line specifications and labor warranty duration. Overall, the pricing appears fair for the Los Angeles area, though getting a second opinion on the tank choice and any necessary upgrades (expansion tank, seismic bracing) would be prudent.

structuredRedFlags.name: no_line_item_breakdown
structuredRedFlags.explanation: Single 'all-in' price with no parts/labor split. Prevents comparison shopping and hides markups on fixtures. Industry standard on any job over $500 is a line-item scope.

data.summary: Straightforward water heater replacement quote from an established LA contractor. All major components itemized: 50-gal Bradford White unit ($1,295), labor at $135/hr for 4 hours ($540), required CA earthquake straps, permit/inspection ($135), and old unit disposal ($85). Expansion tank and safety components included. Warranty solid: 6 years on tank, 1 year on labor. Price appears fair for the market and scope. No red flags; quote is transparent and complete.

Rinnai RU199iN is a quality unit with strong 12-year parts and 5-year labor warranty. Price is on the higher end but reflects full-service scope with two plumbers, multiple upgrades (recirculation pump, smart sensor, seismic safety), and permits included. No red flags on transparency, though gas line safety features (PRV, backflow) not explicitly mentioned — confirm compliance before proceeding.

structuredRedFlags.name: expansion_tank_missing_closed_system
structuredRedFlags.explanation: Water heater replacement on a closed system (PRV or backflow preventer present) requires a thermal expansion tank by code (IPC/UPC). Missing it will fail inspection and can rupture the tank under thermal expansion.
structuredRedFlags.action: Confirm whether home is on a closed system. If yes, require expansion tank line item ($200-$400 installed).

data.summary: Standard gas water heater replacement quote for a 50-gallon Bradford White unit in Los Angeles. Quote is comprehensive and includes all necessary components (earthquake straps per CA code, sediment trap, expansion tank, new connectors, old unit disposal), permit and inspection, and warranty (6 years parts, 1 year labor). Labor rate of $135/hr is reasonable and 4-hour estimate is appropriate for standard installation. Total of $2,553 is mid-to-high range but justified by parts quality, full permitting, code compliance, and inclusion of all accessories. No red flags detected.

lineItems.description: Recirculation pump and dedicated return line

possibleUpsells: Smart leak detection sensor ($295) - may be unnecessary depending on existing water monitoring
Recirculation pump ($895) - increases cost and energy use; necessary only if hot water delivery distance is problematic
Earthquake straps and seismic bracing ($145) - required in CA but should be standard; verify if code-mandated
redFlags: Pipe type for gas line upsize not specified (should verify if copper, PEX, or CSST)
No mention of expansion tank, which is typically required for tankless water heater installations
No specification of venting materials beyond 'stainless steel' — appropriate vent sizing and routing should be confirmed

No state plumbing license number on the quote. Plumbing is licensed statewide in 45+ states. Unlicensed plumbing work voids insurance claims and can't be permitted or inspected. AARP lists unlicensed home services as a top-5 elder fraud category.
data.summary: Tankless water heater replacement quote totaling $7,571 for a Rinnai RU199iN unit with comprehensive installation including gas line upsize, venting, recirculation pump, and smart leak detection. Labor rate of $165/hr for 8 hours (2 plumbers) is reasonable for the Los Angeles area. Quote includes permits and gas inspection. Strong warranty coverage (12 years parts, 5 years labor). However, several best-practice items are not explicitly addressed (expansion tank, detailed pipe/vent specifications, backflow prevention). The recirculation pump and leak detection sensor may be upsells rather than necessities depending on customer needs. Overall pricing appears in line with premium tankless installations in West Hollywood, but recommend verifying all code-required safety items are included.
